summary: Carole Herder blog post recommending Liberated Horsemanship (founded by Dr. Bruce Nock) as a free resource for hoof care, riding, and horse welfare education.
Carole Herder blog post on equine-assisted therapy for veterans with PTSD/TBI, citing Cavallo's sponsorship of PATH and a recovery story of veteran Aaron Heliker.
Customer testimonial about a barefoot Appaloosa gelding named Max who thrives wearing Cavallo Simple Boots on gravel and frozen ground.
Customer testimonial from German endurance rider Nina who has used Cavallo boots for over six years across thousands of miles of endurance riding and trekking.
Carole Herder blog post arguing horses prefer scratching over patting, with bonding and behavioral reasoning and a linked study from TheHorse.com.
Carole Herder announcement welcoming new Cavallo team member Jennifer (Jenny) Mundell, with contact details and product mentions.
Carole Herder blog post on safe winter riding, explaining how metal shoes trap snow/ice and why barefoot hooves plus Cavallo boots and studs improve traction and comfort.
Carole Herder reflective blog post comparing how stress shows in human and horse faces, noting horses display pain and anxiety through facial expression.
Carole Herder blog post on adapting training to a horse's unique personality, referencing the Parelli horsenality system and managing trainer emotions.
Carole Herder blog post on hoof moisture balance, explaining that 90% of hoof moisture comes from within and why most hoof conditioners and oils are ineffective or harmful.
Carole Herder blog post on hoof nerves and blood flow, citing Dr. Robert Bowker on the hoof as a neurosensory organ and the importance of heel-first landing.
Carole Herder blog post detailing types of hoof cracks (grass, sand, heel, bar, toe, quarter), their causes, and how to manage them as the crack grows out.
